hook.text: Dog bites man is news. Man bites dog is bigger news. Word order changes everything in English.

buildup.text: Syntax is the set of rules governing how words combine into phrases and sentences within a language.

discovery.text: Languages use different word orders: English uses subject-verb-object, Japanese uses subject-object-verb.

twist.text: Some languages like Latin rely on word endings, not order, to convey meaning. Syntax flexibility varies enormously.

climax.text: Syntax is the architecture of thought. It determines how speakers organize ideas into communicable structures.

punchline.text: Grammar is the invisible blueprint of thought.

quiz.question: What does syntax govern?
options: How words combine into phrases and sentences
